fields.title: CNA (Certified Nursing Assistant)
fields.description: This intensive, fast-paced program provides a comprehensive introduction to basic nursing care, healthcare systems, and patient advocacy. Students will participate in interactive classroom lectures, simulation lab training, and mandatory supervised clinical rotations in a real-world healthcare facility. The curriculum is meticulously structured to help students master essential competencies, including infection control, measuring vital signs, patient mobility and transfer techniques, nutrition, and psychological care of diverse populations. By completing this program, students are fully equipped with both the cognitive knowledge and the practical, hands-on skills necessary to pass the Oklahoma State Registry exam and confidently step into high-demand, entry-level nursing assistant positions across hospitals, long-term care facilities, and rehabilitation centers.

fields.title: Certified Medication Aide (CMA)
fields.description: Designed specifically for existing Certified Nursing Assistants, this specialized certification program builds upon foundational caregiving skills by training students to safely and accurately administer medications. The rigorous curriculum covers the core principles of pharmacology, standard medical abbreviations, dosage calculations, potential drug interactions, and proper documentation procedures. In addition to classroom instruction, students will practice precise administration techniques for various forms of medicine—including oral, topical, and inhalant medications—under the direct supervision of a licensed nurse. Completion of this program prepares students for the state CMA certification exam, allowing them to take on advanced responsibilities, command higher wages, and play a vital role in maintaining patient health and safety within long-term care and residential facilities.

fields.title: Home Health Aide (HHA)
fields.description: Developed specifically for Certified Nursing Assistants (CNAs) seeking to transition or expand their practice into residential environments, this program equips caregivers with the unique skills needed for independent home-based care. The curriculum highlights the core differences between institutional nursing and home health services, focusing deeply on personal care management, specialized meal preparation tailored to dietary restrictions, light housekeeping to maintain a sterile environment, and adaptive emotional support for homebound patients. Students will learn how to accurately monitor client conditions, manage home safety hazards, and serve as an essential communication link between the patient and their primary medical team. This quick, focused certification opens up flexible employment opportunities with home health agencies, private duty registries, and community-based outreach groups.

fields.title: Basic Life Support (BLS) / CPR
fields.description: This official American Heart Association (AHA) course is engineered to teach healthcare professionals and first responders how to recognize and respond to life-threatening emergencies. Through high-fidelity simulations and hands-on practice, participants learn the mechanics of high-quality cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) for adults, children, and infants. The curriculum also details the rapid deployment of Automated External Defibrillators (AEDs), the effective management of foreign-body airway obstructions (choking), and the utilization of barrier devices such as pocket masks and bag-valve-mask (BVM) ventilation. Led by certified AHA instructors, this program accommodates both first-time certifiers and those looking for card renewal, ensuring everyone leaves with the muscle memory and clinical confidence required to execute crucial lifesaving interventions under pressure.

fields.title: Certified Phlebotomy Technician
fields.description: This extensive training program blends in-depth medical theory with intensive clinical practice to transform students into skilled phlebotomy technicians. Coursework covers core anatomy and physiology—with a particular focus on the circulatory system—along alongside laboratory safety protocols, proper patient identification methods, medical terminology, and correct tube-additives sequencing. Students spend substantial time in the laboratory environment mastering venipuncture, capillary punctures, and proper specimen processing techniques, all while observing strict infection control guidelines. Upon graduation, individuals are fully prepared and eligible to sit for the National Healthcareer Association (NHA) National Certification Exam, paving the way for immediate entry-level employment in high-demand environments like commercial diagnostics labs, blood donation centers, and multi-departmental hospital networks.

fields.title: Certified EKG Technician
fields.description: This technical certification course trains students to work as specialized medical professionals dedicated to cardiovascular health monitoring. The comprehensive curriculum teaches the structural anatomy of the human heart, the fundamentals of cardiac electrophysiology, and advanced medical terminology. Students acquire complete proficiency in utilizing modern diagnostic equipment, focusing heavily on how to properly position 12-lead EKG electrodes, record clear electrical cardiac activity, and isolate standard artifacts. Crucially, the program trains students to spot life-threatening cardiac rhythms, dysrhythmias, and myocardial infarctions, ensuring rapid medical intervention. Graduates will meet all requirements to sit for the NHA Certified EKG Technician (CET) examination, allowing them to secure positions as valued members of diagnostic teams in cardiology practices, emergency rooms, and intensive care units.
